content.full_text:
Re:
Fern PolrrrcAt- Pnecrrcss C orvrMrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramenro, CA 95g14_2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322_0U86
January 5,2009
Laurie Gallian
Councilmember, City of Sonoma
No. I The Plaza
Sonoma, California 9 5 47 6 -6618
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-280
Dear Ms. Gallian:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter should not be construed
as assistance on any conduct that may have already taken place. (See Regulation
18329(bX8)(A).) The Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commis]sion") does not
act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance, and this letter is based on the iacts
presented. (ln re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Also, please note rhar our advice is
based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore ofier no opinion on the
application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of
interest or Government Code Section 1090. This letter serves to confirm telephone
advice that you received on December 30, 2009.
QUESTTON
As a member of the City of Sonoma City Council do you have a conflict of
interest in participating in a decision before the city council rlgarding an appeal of a
decision made by the City of Sonoma Planning Commissior, *h"n your trusUand serves
in an unpaid volunteer member of the planning Commission?
CONCLUSION
No- The Act does not regulate potential conflicts of interest based on personal
relationships. The Act only regulates financial conflicts of interest.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 rhrough gl014. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government Code, untess otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in secrions l8l l0 through l8gg7 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. 4-09-280
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are a member of the City of Sonoma City Council. Your husband serves as
an unpaid volunteer member of the City of Sonoma Planning Commission. On the
upcoming January 6,2009 city council agenda one of the items is an appeal of a planning
commission decision to deny a use permit. The use permit that was denied involved
property that is currently used as a school. The use permit applicant applied for a use
permit that would allow special events such as weddings to Ue tr"ta at ihe school site.
Your husband voted to deny this use permit when it originally came before the planning
Commission. You indicate that you have no financial inlerests involved in this decision.
You would like to h9* if participation in the appeal decision in your capacity as a city
council member would create a conflict of interest due to the relaiionstrip Uetween you
and your husband.
A]\[ALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a govemmental decision in
which the official has a financial interest. A public official lias a "financial interest,, in a
governmental decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the iublic official's
economic interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) You have asked if the
personal relationship between you and your husband creates a conflict of interest under
the Act. The Act does not regulate personal relationships and you have indicated that you
do not have financial interests involved in the decision. Thereiore, you do not have a
potential conflict of interest under the Act with respect to this decisi,on.2
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
d,r//a'K KLo .
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
SKB jgl
2 You have indicated that you do not have any economic interests that could be reasonably
foreseeably materially affected by this decision. Economic interests recognized under the Act include
interests in: business entities, real property, sources of income, sources or-girtr, and your personal finances.
Should you find that you have any economic interests that may be impacte-rl by this decision please contact
us for further assistance.

in an unpaid volunteer member of the planning Commission?
sections.conclusion:
No- The Act does not regulate potential conflicts of interest based on personal
relationships. The Act only regulates financial conflicts of interest.
